title: Microsoft Excel Basics
description: Master the fundamentals of Microsoft Excel. Learn how to navigate the interface, manage workbooks, format data, and perform basic calculations. This certificate course is designed for absolute beginners to build a strong foundation in spreadsheet management.

seo_metadata.title: Microsoft Excel Basics Certificate Course | ProCerix
seo_metadata.description: Learn Microsoft Excel basics from scratch. Earn your certification in spreadsheet management, formatting, and basic formulas.

learning_outcomes: Navigate the Microsoft Excel interface with confidence
Create, save, and manage workbooks and worksheets
Use basic formulas and functions (SUM, AVERAGE, MIN, MAX)
Prepare spreadsheets for professional printing and sharing

modules.title: Getting Started with Excel
modules.description: An introduction to the Excel interface, workbooks, and basic data entry.

lessons.title: Navigating the Excel Interface
lessons.content:
Understanding the Excel Interface

Microsoft Excel is a powerful spreadsheet application used for data management and analysis. In this lesson, we will explore the Ribbon, Quick Access Toolbar, Name Box, and Formula Bar.

The Ribbon

The Ribbon is the primary interface element in Excel, organized into tabs like Home, Insert, Page Layout, and Data. Each tab contains groups of related commands.

Workbooks vs. Worksheets

An Excel file is called a workbook. Each workbook contains one or more worksheets (tabs at the bottom). You can add, rename, and organize these sheets as needed.

lessons.title: Data Entry and Basic Formatting
lessons.content:
Entering and Formatting Data

To enter data, select a cell and start typing. Press Enter to move down, or Tab to move right.

Data Types

Excel recognizes text, numbers, dates, and times. You can format numbers as currency, percentages, or fractions using the Number format dropdown on the Home tab.

Cell Formatting

Use the Font and Alignment groups to change font styles, sizes, colors, and borders to make your data presentable.

lessons.title: Managing Rows and Columns
lessons.content:
Rows, Columns, and Cells

Excel grids are made of columns (letters) and rows (numbers). The intersection is a cell, identified by a reference like A1.

Inserting and Deleting

You can insert or delete rows and columns by right-clicking the column letter or row number and selecting Insert or Delete. This helps in adjusting your spreadsheet structure without losing data.

modules.title: Formulas and Functions
modules.description: Learn how to perform calculations using Excel's built-in tools.

lessons.title: Introduction to Formulas
lessons.content:
Writing Basic Formulas

All formulas in Excel start with an equals sign (=). You can perform standard arithmetic operations: addition (+), subtraction (-), multiplication (*), and division (/).

For example, =A1+B1 adds the values in cells A1 and B1.

lessons.title: Using Basic Functions
lessons.content:
Essential Functions

Functions are predefined formulas. The most common functions include:

  • SUM: Adds a range of cells (e.g., =SUM(A1:A10))
  • AVERAGE: Calculates the average of a range
  • MIN / MAX: Finds the lowest or highest value in a range
  • COUNT: Counts the number of cells containing numbers

lessons.title: Printing and Saving
lessons.content:
Preparing to Print

Before printing, always use Print Preview. You can adjust margins, orientation (Portrait vs. Landscape), and scaling in the Page Layout tab.

Saving Your Work

Save your workbook in the standard .xlsx format. You can also save as a PDF or CSV file depending on your sharing requirements.
